title: Week one of the base year
excerpt: The miles nobody posts — early pool sets, dawn rides, and the quiet work before July.
body: This is the part that doesn't make the feed. Alarm before light, coffee gone cold, legs that haven't decided yet whether they're awake.
Every lap, every mile now is a deposit. July 1 is still a date on the calendar. The work starts anyway.

title: Prescott to Phoenix, by degrees
excerpt: Two towns, one thread — the communities that kept people like me alive.
body: Prescott is where this started. Phoenix is where the miles will land. The desert between them is not empty — it's full of people doing the work.
Cycle for Change is not a stunt. It's a way of staying visible for the orgs that showed up when it mattered.

title: Why 7,500
excerpt: Not round for round's sake — swim, bike, run, and a year that adds up to something real.
body: 100 miles in the water. 7,000 on the bike. 400 on foot. The split follows the disciplines, not a marketing deck.
You pledge a mile. I do the miles. We vote. You give direct. That's the whole machine.
